revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''禰津 元直'''（ねづ もとなお、1495年 - 1575年）[[信濃国]][[小県郡]]禰津を所領とした[[武将]]。根津、祢津。官途は宮内...'
revision.text:
'''禰津 元直'''（ねづ もとなお、1495年 - 1575年）[[信濃国]][[小県郡]]禰津を所領とした[[武将]]。根津、祢津。官途は宮内大輔。常安斎。子に[[根津勝直|勝直]]、[[根津政直|政直]]、[[根津信忠|信忠]]。
1541年、[[武田信虎]]の小県侵攻に破れ、同じ神氏の[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]を通じて知行を安堵された。1542年、娘が[[武田晴信]]に嫁ぎ、[[諏訪御寮人]]と呼ばれた。 1545年に武田臣下となり、政直に家督を譲った。諏訪攻め、[[戸石城]]攻略で功をたて、旧領を回復した。[[岩櫃城]]攻略に加わり、[[箕輪城]]代を勤めた功で上州小鼻を与えられたが[[長篠の戦い]]で討死した。

'''禰津 元直'''（ねづ もとなお、[[1495年]] - [[1575年]]）は、[[信濃国]][[小県郡]]禰津を所領とした[[武将]]。根津、祢津とも。官途は宮内大輔。常安斎。子に[[根津勝直|勝直]]、[[根津政直|政直]]、[[根津信忠|信忠]]。
[[1541年]]、[[武田信虎]]の小県侵攻に破れ、同じ神氏の[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]を通じて知行を安堵された。[[1542年]]、娘が[[武田晴信]]に嫁ぎ、[[諏訪御寮人]]と呼ばれた。
[[1545年]]に武田臣下となり、政直に家督を譲った。諏訪攻め、[[戸石城]]攻略で功をたて、旧領を回復した。[[岩櫃城]]攻略に加わり、[[箕輪城]]代を勤めた功で上州小鼻を与えられたが[[長篠の戦い]]で討死した。
{{DEFAULTSORT:ねつ もとなお}} [[Category:戦国武将]] [[Category:信濃国の人物]] [[Category:1495年生]] [[Category:1575年没]]

'''禰津 元直'''（ねづ もとなお、明応4年（[[1495年]]） - [[天正]]3年（[[1575年]]））は、[[信濃国]][[小県郡]]禰津を所領とした[[武将]]。根津、祢津とも。官途は宮内大輔。常安斎。子に[[根津勝直|勝直]]、[[根津政直|政直]]、[[根津信忠|信忠]]。
[[天文 (元号)|天文]]10年（[[1541年]]）、[[甲斐国]]の[[戦国大名]][[武田信虎]]の小県侵攻に破れ、同じ神氏の[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]を通じて知行を安堵された。[[天文 (元号)|天文]]11年（[[1542年]]）、娘が武田信虎の嫡男[[武田晴信]]に嫁ぎ、[[禰津御寮人]]と呼ばれた。
[[天文 (元号)|天文]]14年（[[1545年]]）に武田臣下となり、政直に家督を譲った。諏訪攻め、[[戸石城]]攻略で功をたて、旧領を回復した。[[岩櫃城]]攻略に加わり、[[箕輪城]]代を勤めた功で上州小鼻を与えられたが[[天正]]3年（[[1575年]]）の[[長篠の戦い]]で討死した。 == 関連項目 == *[[諏訪御料人]] {{DEFAULTSORT:ねつ もとなお}} [[Category:根津氏|もとなお]] [[Category:戦国武将]] [[Category:信濃国の人物]] [[Category:1495年生]] [[Category:1575年没]]

'''禰津 元直'''（ねづ もとなお、[[明応]]4年（[[1495年]]） - [[天正]]3年[[5月21日 (旧暦)|5月21日]]（[[1575年]][[7月9日]]））は、[[信濃国]][[小県郡]]禰津を所領とした[[武将]]。根津、祢津とも。官途は宮内大輔。常安斎。子に[[根津勝直|勝直]]、[[根津政直|政直]]、[[根津信忠|信忠]]。
[[天文 (元号)|天文]]10年（[[1541年]]）、[[甲斐国]]の[[戦国大名]][[武田信虎]]の小県侵攻に破れ、同じ神氏の[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]を通じて知行を安堵された。天文11年（[[1542年]]）、娘が武田信虎の嫡男[[武田晴信]]に嫁ぎ、[[禰津御寮人]]と呼ばれた。
天文14年（[[1545年]]）に武田臣下となり、政直に家督を譲った。諏訪攻め、[[戸石城]]攻略で功をたて、旧領を回復した。[[岩櫃城]]攻略に加わり、[[箕輪城]]代を勤めた功で上州小鼻を与えられたが天正3年（1575年）の[[長篠の戦い]]で討死した。

'''禰津 元直'''（ねづ もとなお、[[明応]]4年（[[1495年]]） - [[天正]]3年[[5月21日 (旧暦)|5月21日]]（[[1575年]][[7月9日]]））は、[[信濃国]][[小県郡]]禰津を所領とした[[武将]]。根津、祢津とも。官途は宮内大輔。常安斎。子に[[根津勝直|勝直]]、[[根津政直|政直]]、[[根津信忠|信忠]]。
[[天文 (元号)|天文]]10年（[[1541年]]）、[[甲斐国]]の[[戦国大名]][[武田信虎]]の小県侵攻に破れ、同じ神氏の[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]を通じて知行を安堵された。天文11年（[[1542年]]）、娘が武田信虎の嫡男[[武田信玄|武田晴信]]に嫁ぎ、[[禰津御寮人]]と呼ばれた。
天文14年（[[1545年]]）に武田臣下となり、政直に家督を譲った。諏訪攻め、[[戸石城]]攻略で功をたて、旧領を回復した。[[岩櫃城]]攻略に加わり、[[箕輪城]]代を勤めた功で上州小鼻を与えられたが天正3年（1575年）の[[長篠の戦い]]で討死した。

'''禰津 元直'''（ねづ もとなお、[[明応]]4年（[[1495年]]） - [[天正]]3年[[5月21日 (旧暦)|5月21日]]（[[1575年]][[7月9日]]））は、[[信濃国]][[小県郡]]禰津を所領とした[[武将]]。根津、祢津とも。官途は[[宮内省|宮内大輔]]。常安斎。子に[[根津勝直|勝直]]、[[根津政直|政直]]、[[根津信忠|信忠]]。
[[天文 (元号)|天文]]10年（[[1541年]]）、[[甲斐国]]の[[戦国大名]][[武田信虎]]の小県侵攻に破れ、同じ神氏の[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]を通じて知行を安堵された。天文11年（[[1542年]]）、娘が武田信虎の嫡男[[武田信玄|武田晴信]]に嫁ぎ、[[禰津御寮人]]と呼ばれた。
天文14年（[[1545年]]）に武田臣下となり、政直に家督を譲った。諏訪攻め、[[戸石城]]攻略で功をたて、旧領を回復した。[[岩櫃城]]攻略に加わり、[[箕輪城]]代を勤めた功で上州小鼻を与えられたが天正3年（1575年）の[[長篠の戦い]]で討死した。
== 関連項目 == *[[諏訪御料人]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:ねつ もとなお}} [[Category:根津氏|もとなお]] [[Category:戦国武将]] [[Category:信濃国の人物]] [[Category:戦死した人物]] [[Category:1495年生]] [[Category:1575年没]]

'''禰津 元直'''（ねづ もとなお、[[明応]]4年（[[1495年]]） - [[天正]]3年[[5月21日 (旧暦)|5月21日]]（[[1575年]][[7月9日]]））は、[[信濃国]][[小県郡]]禰津を所領とした[[武将]]。根津、祢津とも。官途は[[宮内省|宮内大輔]]。常安斎。子に[[根津勝直|勝直]]、[[根津政直|政直]]、[[根津信忠|信忠]]。
[[天文 (元号)|天文]]10年（[[1541年]]）、[[甲斐国]]の[[戦国大名]][[武田信虎]]の小県侵攻に破れ、同じ神氏の[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]を通じて知行を安堵された。『[[高白斎記]]』天文11年（[[1542年]]）12月15日条に拠れば、娘が武田信虎の嫡男[[武田信玄|武田晴信]]に嫁ぎ、[[禰津御寮人]]と呼ばれた。『高白斎記』天文11年条には「禰津より御前様」が輿入れしたとされているが、これは禰津御寮人に比定する説のほか、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の娘である[[諏訪御料人]]に比定する説もある。
天文14年（[[1545年]]）に武田臣下となり、政直に家督を譲った。諏訪攻め、[[戸石城]]攻略で功をたて、旧領を回復した。[[岩櫃城]]攻略に加わり、[[箕輪城]]代を勤めた功で上州小鼻を与えられたが天正3年（1575年）の[[長篠の戦い]]で討死した。
== 関連項目 == *[[諏訪御料人]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:ねつ もとなお}} [[Category:根津氏|もとなお]] [[Category:戦国武将]] [[Category:信濃国の人物]] [[Category:戦死した人物]] [[Category:1495年生]] [[Category:1575年没]]

'''禰津 元直'''（ねつ もとなお、生没年不詳<ref>丸島（2015）、p.561</ref>）は、戦国時代の国衆・禰津氏の当主<ref>丸島（2015）、p.561</ref>。禰津氏は[[信濃国]][[小県郡]]禰津（[[長野県]][[東御市]]）を本拠とした<ref>丸島（2015）、p.561</ref>。姓は根津、祢津とも。官途は[[宮内省|宮内大輔]]<ref>丸島（2015）、p.561</ref>。法名は「元山」<ref>丸島（2015）、p.561</ref>。子に[[根津勝直|勝直]]、[[根津常安|常安]]、[[根津信忠|信忠]]。
[[天文 (元号)|天文]]10年（[[1541年]]）、[[甲斐国]]の[[戦国大名]]・[[武田信虎]]の小県侵攻に破れ、同じ神氏の[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]を通じて知行を安堵された。
『[[高白斎記]]』天文11年（[[1543年]]）12月15日条に拠れば、娘が武田信虎の嫡男・[[武田信玄|武田晴信]]（信玄）の[[側室]]として嫁ぎ、「[[禰津御寮人]]」と呼ばれた<ref>丸島（2015）、p.561</ref>。『高白斎記』天文11年条には「禰津より御前様」が輿入れしたとされているが、これは禰津御寮人に比定する説のほか、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の娘である[[諏訪御料人]]に比定する説もある。
天文11年には甲斐武田氏の家臣となる<ref>丸島（2015）、p.561</ref>。天文22年8月16日には信濃庄内（長野県[[坂城町]]）に1000貫文を与えられる<ref>丸島（2015）、p.561</ref>。元直の子息のうち勝直は早世し、次男の常安（実名は「政秀」か）が家督を継承する<ref>丸島（2015）、p.561</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 参考文献 == * 丸島和洋「禰津元直」柴辻俊六・平山優・黒田基樹・丸島和洋編『武田氏家臣団人名辞典』東京堂出版、2015年
== 関連項目 == *[[諏訪御料人]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:ねつ もとなお}} [[Category:根津氏|もとなお]] [[Category:戦国武将]] [[Category:信濃国の人物]]

[[禰津御寮人]]（[[武田信玄]]側室） | 特記事項 = }} '''禰津 元直'''（ねつ/ねづ もとなお）は、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の[[武将]]。姓は根津、祢津とも。 [[禰津氏]]は[[信濃国]][[小県郡]]禰津（[[長野県]][[東御市]]）を本拠とした[[国人]]。 [[天文 (元号)|天文]]10年（[[1541年]]）、[[甲斐国]]の[[戦国大名]]・[[武田信虎]]の小県侵攻（[[海野平の戦い]]）に敗れたが、同じ[[諏訪神党]]の[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]を通じて武田氏に臣従して、[[武田信廉]]に附属し、知行を安堵された。 『[[高白斎記]]』天文11年（[[1542年]]）12月15日条に拠れば、娘が武田信虎の嫡男・[[武田信玄|武田晴信]]（信玄）の[[側室]]として嫁ぎ、「[[禰津御寮人]]」と呼ばれた。『高白斎記』天文11年条には「禰津より御前様」が輿入れしたとされているが、これは禰津御寮人に比定する説のほか、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の娘である[[諏訪御料人]]に比定する説もある。 天文22年（1553年）8月16日には信濃庄内（長野県[[坂城町]]）に1000貫文を与えられる。元直の子息のうち[[禰津勝直|勝直]]は早世したため、次男・[[禰津常安|常安]]（実名は「政秀」か）が家督を継承する。 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == * [[丸島和洋]]「禰津元直」[[柴辻俊六]]・[[平山優 (歴史学者)|平山優]]・[[黒田基樹]]・丸島和洋編『武田氏家臣団人名辞典』東京堂出版、2015年 * 田中豊茂「信濃中世武家伝」[[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] 2016年 {{DEFAULTSORT:ねつ もとなお}} [[Category:根津氏|もとなお]] [[Category:戦国武将]] [[Category:信濃国の人物]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 禰津元直 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]] | 生誕 = 不明丸島（2015）、p.561 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = 元直→元山（法名） | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 霊名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[宮内省|宮内大輔]]（受領名） | 幕府 = | 主君 = [[武田信虎]] [[武田晴信]] | 藩 = | 氏族 = [[禰津氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[禰津覚直]] | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = [[禰津勝直|勝直]]、[[根津政直]]、[[禰津信忠|信忠]]、
[[禰津御寮人]]（[[武田信玄]]側室） | 特記事項 = }} '''禰津 元直'''（ねつ/ねづ もとなお）は、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の[[武将]]。姓は根津、祢津とも。 [[禰津氏]]は[[信濃国]][[小県郡]]禰津（[[長野県]][[東御市]]）を本拠とした[[国人]]。 [[天文 (元号)|天文]]10年（[[1541年]]）、[[甲斐国]]の[[戦国大名]]・[[武田信虎]]の小県侵攻（[[海野平の戦い]]）に敗れたが、同じ[[諏訪神党]]の[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]を通じて武田氏に臣従して、[[武田信廉]]に附属し、知行を安堵された。 『[[高白斎記]]』天文11年（[[1542年]]）12月15日条に拠れば、娘が武田信虎の嫡男・[[武田信玄|武田晴信]]（信玄）の[[側室]]として嫁ぎ、「[[禰津御寮人]]」と呼ばれた。『高白斎記』天文11年条には「禰津より御前様」が輿入れしたとされているが、これは禰津御寮人に比定する説のほか、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の娘である[[諏訪御料人]]に比定する説もある。 天文22年（1553年）8月16日には信濃庄内（長野県[[坂城町]]）に1000貫文を与えられる。元直の子息のうち[[禰津勝直|勝直]]は早世したため、次男・[[禰津常安|政直]]（出家名：松鴎軒常安）が家督を継承する。 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == * [[丸島和洋]]「禰津元直」[[柴辻俊六]]・[[平山優 (歴史学者)|平山優]]・[[黒田基樹]]・丸島和洋編『武田氏家臣団人名辞典』東京堂出版、2015年 * 田中豊茂「信濃中世武家伝」[[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] 2016年 {{DEFAULTSORT:ねつ もとなお}} [[Category:根津氏|もとなお]] [[Category:戦国武将]] [[Category:信濃国の人物]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox samurai | name = Netsu Motonao (禰津元直) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} | birth_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561">Marushima (2015), p.561</ref> | death_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561"/> | other_names = Motonao → Motoyama (posthumous name)<ref name="maru561"/> | aliases = | titles = {{ill|Kunaidaibu|ja|宮内大輔}} (official title)<ref name="maru561"/> | allegiance = {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}, {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} | clan = {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Netsu Sadanobu|ja|禰津覚直}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}}, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|根津政直}}, {{ill|Netsu Nobutaka|ja|禰津信忠}}, {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}} (concubine of Takeda Shingen) }}
'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|侍}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>

== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>

However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>

{{Infobox samurai | name = Netsu Motonao (禰津元直) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} | birth_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561">Marushima (2015), p.561</ref> | death_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561"/> | other_names = Motonao → Motoyama (posthumous name)<ref name="maru561"/> | aliases = | titles = {{ill|Kunaidaibu|ja|宮内大輔}} (official title)<ref name="maru561"/> | allegiance = {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}, {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} | clan = {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Netsu Sadanobu|ja|禰津覚直}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}}, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|根津政直}}, {{ill|Netsu Nobutaka|ja|禰津信忠}}, {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}} (concubine of Takeda Shingen) }}
'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|侍}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

{{Infobox samurai | name = Netsu Motonao (禰津元直) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} | birth_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561">Marushima (2015), p.561</ref> | death_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561"/> | other_names = Motonao → Motoyama (posthumous name)<ref name="maru561"/> | aliases = | titles = {{ill|Kunaidaibu|ja|宮内大輔}} (official title)<ref name="maru561"/> | allegiance = {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}, {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} | clan = {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Netsu Sadanobu|ja|禰津覚直}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}}, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|根津政直}}, {{ill|Netsu Nobutaka|ja|禰津信忠}}, {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}} (concubine of Takeda Shingen) }}
'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|侍}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|侍}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

revision.text:
{{Infobox samurai | name = Netsu Motonao (禰津元直) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} | birth_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561">Marushima (2015), p.561</ref> | death_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561"/> | other_names = Motonao → Motoyama (posthumous name)<ref name="maru561"/> | aliases = | titles = {{ill|Kunaidaibu|ja|宮内大輔}} (official title)<ref name="maru561"/> | allegiance = {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}, {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} | clan = {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Netsu Sadanobu|ja|禰津覚直}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}}, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|根津政直}}, {{ill|Netsu Nobutaka|ja|禰津信忠}}, {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}} (concubine of Takeda Shingen) }}
'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|侍}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|侍}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|侍}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

{{Infobox samurai | name = Netsu Motonao (禰津元直) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} | birth_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561">Marushima (2015), p.561</ref> | death_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561"/> | other_names = Motonao → Motoyama (posthumous name)<ref name="maru561"/> | aliases = | titles = {{ill|Kunaidaibu|ja|宮内大輔}} (official title)<ref name="maru561"/> | allegiance = {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}, {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} | clan = {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Netsu Sadanobu|ja|禰津覚直}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}}, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|根津政直}}, {{ill|Netsu Nobutaka|ja|禰津信忠}}, {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}} (concubine of Takeda Shingen) }}
'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|侍}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

{{Infobox samurai | name = Netsu Motonao (禰津元直) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} | birth_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561">Marushima (2015), p.561</ref> | death_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561"/> | other_names = Motonao → Motoyama (posthumous name)<ref name="maru561"/> | aliases = | titles = {{ill|Kunaidaibu|ja|宮内大輔}} (official title)<ref name="maru561"/> | allegiance = {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}, {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} | clan = {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Netsu Sadanobu|ja|禰津覚直}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}}, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|根津政直}}, {{ill|Netsu Nobutaka|ja|禰津信忠}}, {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}} (concubine of Takeda Shingen) }}
'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|侍}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun qq (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

{{Infobox samurai | name = Netsu Motonao (禰津元直) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} | birth_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561">Marushima (2015), p.561</ref> | death_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561"/> | other_names = Motonao → Motoyama (posthumous name)<ref name="maru561"/> | aliases = | titles = {{ill|Kunaidaibu|ja|宮内大輔}} (official title)<ref name="maru561"/> | allegiance = {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}, {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}} | clan = {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Netsu Sadanobu|ja|禰津覚直}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}}, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|根津政直}}, {{ill|Netsu Nobutaka|ja|禰津信忠}}, {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}} (concubine of Takeda Shingen) }}
'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|侍}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun qq (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|侍}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun qq (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>

'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|侍}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

{{Infobox samurai | name = Netsu Motonao (禰津元直) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} | birth_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561">Marushima (2015), p.561</ref> | death_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561"/> | other_names = Motonao → Motoyama (posthumous name)<ref name="maru561"/> | aliases = | titles = {{ill|Kunaidaibu|ja|宮内大輔}} (official title)<ref name="maru561"/> | allegiance = {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}, {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}} | clan = {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Netsu Sadanobu|ja|禰津覚直}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}}, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|根津政直}}, {{ill|Netsu Nobutaka|ja|禰津信忠}}, {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}} (concubine of Takeda Shingen) }}
'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|侍}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

revision.text:
{{Infobox samurai | name = Netsu Motonao (禰津元直) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} | birth_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561">Marushima (2015), p.561</ref> | death_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561"/> | other_names = Motonao → Motoyama (posthumous name)<ref name="maru561"/> | aliases = | titles = {{ill|Kunaidaibu|ja|宮内大輔}} (official title)<ref name="maru561"/> | allegiance = {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}, {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}} | clan = {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Netsu Sadanobu|ja|禰津覚直}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}}, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|根津政直}}, {{ill|Netsu Nobutaka|ja|禰津信忠}}, {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}} (concubine of Takeda Shingen) }}
'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|侍}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|12=draft}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|12=draft}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|12=draft}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|12=draft}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016. <!--Netsu Motonao-->
<!--Netsu Motonao-->

revision.text:
{{Infobox samurai | name = Netsu Motonao (禰津元直) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|12=draft}} | birth_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561">Marushima (2015), p.561</ref> | death_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561"/> | other_names = Motonao → Motoyama (posthumous name)<ref name="maru561"/> | aliases = | titles = {{ill|Kunaidaibu|ja|宮内大輔|12=draft}} (official title)<ref name="maru561"/> | allegiance = {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|12=draft}} | clan = {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|12=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Netsu Sadanobu|ja|禰津覚直|12=draft}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|12=draft}}, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|根津政直|12=draft}}, {{ill|Netsu Nobutaka|ja|禰津信忠|12=draft}}, {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|12=draft}} (concubine of Takeda Shingen) }}
'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|侍|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|12=draft}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏|12=draft}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|12=draft}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|12=draft}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津|12=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市|12=draft}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=draft}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎|12=draft}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い|12=draft}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重|12=draft}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉|12=draft}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記|12=draft}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|12=draft}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|12=draft}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|12=draft}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|12=draft}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|12=draft}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|12=draft}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|12=draft}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016. <!--Netsu Motonao-->
<!--Netsu Motonao-->

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 禰津元直 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]] | 生誕 = 不明丸島（2015）、p.561 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = 元直→元山（法名） | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 霊名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[宮内省 (律令制)|宮内大輔]]（受領名） | 幕府 = | 主君 = [[武田信虎]] [[武田信玄|武田晴信]] | 藩 = | 氏族 = [[禰津氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[禰津覚直]] | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = [[禰津勝直|勝直]]、[[根津政直]]、[[禰津信忠|信忠]]、
[[禰津御寮人]]（[[武田信玄]]側室） | 特記事項 = }} '''禰津 元直'''（ねつ/ねづ もとなお）は、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の[[武将]]。姓は根津、祢津とも。 [[禰津氏]]は[[信濃国]][[小県郡]]禰津（[[長野県]][[東御市]]）を本拠とした[[国人]]。 [[天文 (元号)|天文]]10年（[[1541年]]）、[[甲斐国]]の[[戦国大名]]・[[武田信虎]]の小県侵攻（[[海野平の戦い]]）に敗れたが、同じ[[諏訪神党]]の[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]を通じて武田氏に臣従して、[[武田信廉]]に附属し、知行を安堵された。 『[[高白斎記]]』天文11年（[[1542年]]）12月15日条に拠れば、娘が武田信虎の嫡男・[[武田信玄|武田晴信]]（信玄）の[[側室]]として嫁ぎ、「[[禰津御寮人]]」と呼ばれた。『高白斎記』天文11年条には「禰津より御前様」が輿入れしたとされているが、これは禰津御寮人に比定する説のほか、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の娘である[[諏訪御料人]]に比定する説もある。 天文22年（1553年）8月16日には信濃庄内（長野県[[坂城町]]）に1000貫文を与えられる。元直の子息のうち[[禰津勝直|勝直]]は早世したため、次男・[[禰津常安|政直]]（別名政秀とも。出家名：[[禰津常安|松鴎軒常安]]）が家督を継承する。 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == * [[丸島和洋]]「禰津元直」[[柴辻俊六]]・[[平山優 (歴史学者)|平山優]]・[[黒田基樹]]・丸島和洋編『武田氏家臣団人名辞典』東京堂出版、2015年 * 田中豊茂「信濃中世武家伝」[[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] 2016年 {{DEFAULTSORT:ねつ もとなお}} [[Category:戦国武将]] [[Category:信濃国人領主]] [[Category:根津氏|もとなお]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|qq=}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|qq=}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|qq=}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />

{{Infobox samurai | name = Netsu Motonao (禰津元直) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|qq=}} | birth_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561">Marushima (2015), p.561</ref> | death_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561"/> | other_names = Motonao → Motoyama (posthumous name)<ref name="maru561"/> | aliases = | titles = {{ill|Kunaidaibu|ja|宮内大輔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunaidaibu|qq=}} (official title)<ref name="maru561"/> | allegiance = {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Nobutora|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=}} | clan = {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|qq=}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Netsu Sadanobu|ja|禰津覚直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Sadanobu|qq=}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|qq=}}, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|根津政直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|qq=}}, {{ill|Netsu Nobutaka|ja|禰津信忠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Nobutaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|qq=}} (concubine of Takeda Shingen) }}
'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|samurai|ja|侍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|qq=}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda clan|qq=}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|qq=}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|qq=}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|qq=}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmi, Nagano|qq=}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Nobutora|qq=}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Uedahara|qq=}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yorishige|qq=}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|ja|武田信廉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Nobukado|qq=}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahakasaiki|qq=}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|qq=}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|qq=}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|qq=}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|qq=}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|qq=}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|qq=}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|qq=}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|qq=}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|qq=}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|qq=}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|qq=}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>

'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a [[:en:Samurai|samurai]] of the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]], known for his service to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]]. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|qq=}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|qq=}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|qq=}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmi, Nagano|qq=}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=}} 10 (1541), during [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]]'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Uedahara|qq=}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of [[:en:Takeda Nobukado|Takeda Nobukado]] and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahakasaiki|qq=}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|qq=}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|qq=}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|qq=}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.

== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|qq=}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|qq=}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|qq=}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|qq=}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|qq=}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|qq=}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>

'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a [[:en:Samurai|samurai]] of the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]], known for his service to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]]. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|qq=}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|qq=}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|qq=}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmi, Nagano|qq=}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=}} 10 (1541), during [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]]'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Uedahara|qq=}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of [[:en:Takeda Nobukado|Takeda Nobukado]] and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahakasaiki|qq=}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|qq=}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|qq=}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.

'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a [[:en:Samurai|samurai]] of the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]], known for his service to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]]. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 根津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|qq=}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmi, Nagano|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=}} 10 (1541), during [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]]'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Uedahara|qq=}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of [[:en:Takeda Nobukado|Takeda Nobukado]] and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahakasaiki|qq=}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|qq=}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|qq=}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>

== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11594497]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox samurai | name = Netsu Motonao (禰津元直) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]] | birth_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561">Marushima (2015), p.561</ref> | death_date = Unknown<ref name="maru561"/> | other_names = Motonao → Motoyama (posthumous name)<ref name="maru561"/> | aliases = | titles = {{ill|Kunaidaibu|ja|宮内大輔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunaidaibu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内省}} (official title)<ref name="maru561"/> | allegiance = [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]], {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | clan = {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 根津氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Netsu Sadanobu|ja|禰津覚直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Sadanobu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|根津政直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禰津常安}}, {{ill|Netsu Nobutaka|ja|禰津信忠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Nobutaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|qq=}} (concubine of Takeda Shingen) }}
'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a [[:en:Samurai|samurai]] of the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]], known for his service to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]]. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 根津氏}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|qq=}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmi, Nagano|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=}} 10 (1541), during [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]]'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Uedahara|qq=}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of [[:en:Takeda Nobukado|Takeda Nobukado]] and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahakasaiki|qq=}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|qq=}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|qq=}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

based on wikidata|WD=Q11536451}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Netsu Sadanobu|ja|禰津覚直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Sadanobu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|根津政直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禰津常安|1=Nezu Masanao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536446}}, {{ill|Netsu Nobutaka|ja|禰津信忠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Nobutaka|qq=|1=Netsu Nobutaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95163316}}, {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|qq=|1=Nezugoryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594499}} (concubine of Takeda Shingen) }}
'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a [[:en:Samurai|samurai]] of the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]], known for his service to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]]. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 根津氏|1=Netsu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536451}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|qq=|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmi, Nagano|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 10 (1541), during [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]]'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Uedahara|qq=|1=Battle of Unnodaira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559353}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of [[:en:Takeda Nobukado|Takeda Nobukado]] and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahakasaiki|qq=|1=Kōhakusai-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3335898}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄|1=Takeda Shingen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276404}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|qq=|1=Nezugoryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594499}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|qq=|1=Suwa Goryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193299}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.
{{translated page|ja|禰津元直|version=100231650|comment=Imported full ja history}}
