'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a [[:en:Samurai|samurai]] of the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]], known for his service to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]]. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 根津氏|1=Netsu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536451}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|qq=|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmi, Nagano|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 10 (1541), during [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]]'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Uedahara|qq=|1=Battle of Unnodaira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559353}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of [[:en:Takeda Nobukado|Takeda Nobukado]] and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahakasaiki|qq=|1=Kōhakusai-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3335898}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄|1=Takeda Shingen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276404}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|qq=|1=Nezugoryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594499}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|qq=|1=Suwa Goryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193299}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a [[:en:Samurai|samurai]] of the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]], known for his service to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]]. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 根津氏|1=Netsu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536451}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmi, Nagano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 10 (1541), during [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]]'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Uedahara|1=Battle of Unnodaira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559353}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of [[:en:Takeda Nobukado|Takeda Nobukado]] and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahakasaiki|1=Kōhakusai-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3335898}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄|1=Takeda Shingen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276404}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|1=Nezugoryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594499}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|1=Suwa Goryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193299}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

Goryōnin|1=Nezugoryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594499}} (concubine of Takeda Shingen) }}
'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a [[:en:Samurai|samurai]] of the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]], known for his service to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]]. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 根津氏|1=Netsu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536451}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmi, Nagano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 10 (1541), during [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]]'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Uedahara|1=Battle of Unnodaira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559353}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of [[:en:Takeda Nobukado|Takeda Nobukado]] and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahakasaiki|1=Kōhakusai-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3335898}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄|1=Takeda Shingen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276404}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|1=Nezugoryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594499}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|1=Suwa Goryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193299}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a [[:en:Samurai|samurai]] of the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]], known for his service to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]]. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 根津氏|1=Netsu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536451}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmi, Nagano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 10 (1541), during [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]]'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Uedahara|1=Battle of Unnodaira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559353}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of [[:en:Takeda Nobukado|Takeda Nobukado]] and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahakasaiki|1=Kōhakusai-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3335898}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄|1=Takeda Shingen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276404}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|1=Nezugoryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594499}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|1=Suwa Goryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193299}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a [[:en:Samurai|samurai]] of the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]], known for his service to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]]. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 根津氏|1=Netsu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536451}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmi, Nagano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 10 (1541), during [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]]'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Uedahara|1=Battle of Unnodaira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559353}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of [[:en:Takeda Nobukado|Takeda Nobukado]] and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahakasaiki|1=Kōhakusai-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3335898}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄|1=Takeda Shingen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276404}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|1=Nezugoryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594499}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|1=Suwa Goryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193299}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.

'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a [[:en:Samurai|samurai]] of the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period (Japan)]], known for his service to the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]]. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 根津氏|1=Netsu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536451}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmi, Nagano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 10 (1541), during [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]]'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Uedahara|1=Battle of Unnodaira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559353}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of [[:en:Takeda Nobukado|Takeda Nobukado]] and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahakasaiki|1=Kōhakusai-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3335898}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄|1=Takeda Shingen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276404}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|1=Nezugoryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594499}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|1=Suwa Goryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193299}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.
{{translated page|ja|禰津元直|version=100231650|comment=Imported full ja history}}

His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 根津氏|1=Netsu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536451}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmi, Nagano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|en|Takeda Nobutora|WD=Q1192628|lt=Takeda Nobutora}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Uedahara|1=Battle of Unnodaira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559353}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|en|Takeda Nobukado|WD=Q1992978|lt=Takeda Nobukado}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahakasaiki|1=Kōhakusai-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3335898}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄|1=Takeda Shingen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276404}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|1=Nezugoryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594499}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|1=Suwa Goryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193299}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.
{{translated page|ja|禰津元直|version=100231650|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=samurai}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|en|Takeda clan|WD=Q1210114|lt=Takeda clan}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 根津氏|1=Netsu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536451}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmi, Nagano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|en|Takeda Nobutora|WD=Q1192628|lt=Takeda Nobutora}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Uedahara|1=Battle of Unnodaira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559353}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|en|Takeda Nobukado|WD=Q1992978|lt=Takeda Nobukado}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahakasaiki|1=Kōhakusai-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3335898}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄|1=Takeda Shingen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276404}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|1=Nezugoryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594499}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|1=Suwa Goryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193299}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.
{{translated page|ja|禰津元直|version=100231650|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Netsu Motonao''' (禰津元直) was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=samurai|1=Samurai}} of the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku period}}, known for his service to the {{ill|Takeda clan|en|Takeda clan|WD=Q1210114|lt=Takeda clan|1=Takeda clan}}. His family name is also written as Nezu or Nesu.
== Overview == The {{ill|Netsu clan|ja|禰津氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 根津氏|1=Q11536451|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536451}} was a {{ill|kokujin|ja|国人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kokujin|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} clan based in {{ill|Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja|信濃国小県郡禰津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu, Chiisagata District, Shinano Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (modern-day {{ill|Tōmi, Nagano|ja|長野県東御市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōmi, Nagano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/>
In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 10 (1541), during {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|en|Takeda Nobutora|WD=Q1192628|lt=Takeda Nobutora|1=Takeda Nobutora}}'s invasion of Chiisagata (the {{ill|Battle of Uedahara|ja|海野平の戦い|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Uedahara|1=Battle of Unnodaira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559353}}), the Netsu clan was defeated. However, through mediation by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige}}, a fellow member of the Suwa Shrine priestly lineage, Motonao submitted to the Takeda clan. He was placed under the supervision of {{ill|Takeda Nobukado|en|Takeda Nobukado|WD=Q1992978|lt=Takeda Nobukado|1=Takeda Nobukado}} and retained his lands.<ref name="maru561"/>
According to the ''{{ill|Takahakasaiki|ja|高白斎記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahakasaiki|1=Kōhakusai-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3335898}},'' on the 15th day of the 12th month in Tenbun 11 (1542), one of Motonao's daughters married {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄|1=Takeda Shingen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276404}} (later Takeda Shingen) as a concubine. She was known as {{ill|Netsu Goryōnin|ja|禰津御寮人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Goryōnin|1=Nezugoryōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594499}}.<ref name="maru561"/> The ''Takahakasaiki'' mentions a "person from Netsu" entering the Takeda household, a description traditionally attributed to Netsu Goryōnin. However, some scholars argue this might refer to {{ill|Suwa Goryōnin|ja|諏訪御料人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Goryōnin|1=Suwa Goronin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193299}}, a daughter of Suwa Yorishige.
In Tenbun 22 (1553), Motonao was granted an estate worth 1,000 ''kanmon'' in the Shōnai area (modern-day {{ill|Sakaki, Nagano|ja|長野県坂城町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaki, Nagano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).<ref name="maru561"/> Among his children, {{ill|Netsu Katsunao|ja|禰津勝直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Katsunao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} predeceased him, so the family leadership passed to his second son, {{ill|Netsu Masanao|ja|禰津政直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Netsu Masanao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as Masahide or by his monastic name, Matsōken Jōan).<ref name="maru561"/>
== References == <references />
== Further Reading == * Marushima Kazuhiro, "Netsu Motonao," in {{ill|Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja|武田氏家臣団人名辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Clan Retainers Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, edited by Shibazuki Toshinobu, Hirayama Masaru, Kuroda Motoki, and Marushima Kazuhiro, Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 2015. * Tanaka Toyoshige, ''Shinano Chūsei Buke-den,'' Shinano Mainichi Shimbunsha, 2016.
{{translated page|ja|禰津元直|version=100231650|comment=Imported full ja history}}
